pundit.name: Eric Wynalda
pundit.take: **Ghana will dismantle England today, and it'll be as embarrassing as "The Backpass Assassin's" joke of a draft.** England might have a strong roster, but Gayden passed on nations with real potential, picking with all the precision of a no-look backpass into his own net. Meanwhile, "Mustard Boy" Zach, with teams like Brazil and Ghana, at least shows some semblance of footballing knowledge – though even Austria let him down against Argentina yesterday. Prediction: Expect Ghana to capitalize on England's shaky defense, proving once again why Gayden is hopelessly out of his depth, clinging to that last-place spot in the standings with 13 measly points.

pundit_takes.headline: Mustard Boy's Austria Proves As Tangy As Plain Bread
pundit_takes.subtitle: Zach's Tier 2 squad fails to score, earns zero points.

pundit_takes.headline: Backpass Assassin Poised to Collide with Mustard Boy's Spicy Ghana
pundit_takes.subtitle: Gayden's England faces Zach's Ghana; neither manager looks prepared.

pundit_takes.headline: Colombia Set to Challenge Bubba G's Congo in Late-Night Showdown
pundit_takes.subtitle: Gunner relies on underdog magic as DR Congo hits the field.

pundit_takes.headline: Teen Sensation The Noisemaker Ties for First Without Winning a Game
pundit_takes.subtitle: Rafe's stunning strategy of drafting non-qualifiers confounds experts.

rome_analytics_quotes: Gunner and Rafe tied—no fireworks, just fizzles at the top.
Rafe's Tier-1 dependency screams the kid’s just coasting on Germany.
Devin looks like Rafe's personal punching bag with that -6 differential.
The model's probability predictions couldn't predict Rafe crashing the league.
